question: Iran closes its airspace by May 31st?
newsShortReason: Iran has recently imposed partial airspace closures amid escalating tensions with the U.S.
newsKeyEvidence: On May 22, 2026, Iran closed western airspace to non-day flights until May 25, 2026, amid heightened tensions with the U.S. | On May 23, 2026, Iran's Civil Aviation Organization announced temporary restrictions on the western part of the country's airspace. | On May 26, 2026, Iran condemned recent U.S. airstrikes as a display of 'bad faith' amid ongoing negotiations. | As of May 27, 2026, Iran's international civilian airspace remains closed, with domestic flights operating under restrictions. | The U.S. imposed a naval blockade on Iran from April 13, 2026, leading to significant economic losses for Iran. | The U.S. conducted airstrikes on Iran on May 7, 2026, targeting military sites in southern Iran and Tehran. | Iran has demonstrated both the capability and political will to shut its skies, having done so twice in the past two years. | The market's implied probability of a 47.5% chance of airspace closure by May 31, 2026, reflects a live threat. | The resolution criteria require a closure affecting at least two of five major airports, not partial restrictions or weather closures. | The market's implied probability of a 47.5% chance of airspace closure by May 31, 2026, reflects a live threat.
